channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Criticism mounts over Labor’s rushed tax overhaul amid lack of consultation
item.description: The article examines the controversy surrounding the Albanese government’s rapid push to implement significant tax reforms, arguing that the process has been driven more by political considerations th...

item.title: Surveillance footage reveals illegal industrial waste dumping in Melbourne’s north-east
item.description: Surveillance footage gathered for Nillumbik Shire Council has revealed extensive illegal dumping of industrial waste in the Diamond Creek area in Melbourne’s north-east. The material, reportedly disca...

item.title: Flood watch issued for Banff National Park as snowmelt raises river levels
item.description: Authorities have issued a flood watch and high streamflow advisories for parts of Banff National Park in Alberta due to rapid snowmelt driving rising river levels. The advisories affect the Pipestone ...

item.title: African scholars call for universities to prioritise African-centred knowledge production and intellectual independence
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, scholars and policymakers gathered at the 17th Julius Nyerere International Festival held at the University of Dar es Salaam (UDSM) to discuss Africa’s role in global knowledge produ...

item.title: Ousmane Dembélé’s resurgence and key role for France ahead of the 2026 World Cup
item.description: Ousmane Dembélé has emerged as one of the key figures for France heading into the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with renewed attention on his career revival after years of inconsistency and unfulfilled potenti...

item.title: Austrian Court Jails Man 15 Years for Planned IS-Linked Attack on Taylor Swift Concert
item.description: An Austrian court has sentenced a 21-year-old man, identified as Beran A., to 15 years in prison after he was found guilty of planning a jihadist-inspired terror attack targeting a Taylor Swift concer...

item.title: A couple shares their journey of living full-time on cruise ships
item.description: Until the age of 47, the narrator had little interest in cruises, associating them with nausea from even short boat trips. In 2019, a friend's unused Caribbean cruise presented an opportunity that the...

item.title: AI leaders soften warnings about mass job losses as evidence of disruption remains limited
item.description: Leading figures in artificial intelligence are revising earlier warnings about an imminent wave of mass job losses caused by AI. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman recently said he is “delighted to be wrong” about...

item.title: Marcos dismisses rumours of Jonvic Remulla removal, suggests political interest behind speculation
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has dismissed circulating rumours about the alleged removal or sacking of Interior and Local Government Secretary Jonvic Remulla, clarifying that there is no basis for t...

item.title: British Columbia man sets two Guinness World Records for tree planting in 24 hours
item.description: A man from British Columbia has been recognised by Guinness World Records for achieving two records related to tree planting within a single 24-hour period. According to the report, the Vancouver-area...

item.title: Opinion piece claims Western governments are intensifying crackdowns on activism and journalism
item.description: The article is an opinion commentary by Caitlin Johnstone arguing that Western governments and allied institutions are increasingly restricting activism and journalism under the guise of national secu...

item.title: Authorities Struggle to Identify All Victims After Utumishi Girls Academy Dormitory Fire
item.description: Kenya is still in shock after a tragic fire ripped through the dormitory at Utumishi Girls Academy in Nakuru County, leaving 16 students dead. As of now, only seven of the victims have been positively...

item.title: Preparing Tanzania for the Future of Precision Medicine and Genomics
item.description: For too long, Africa has often arrived late to major scientific revolutions, missing opportunities in industrial, digital, and technological progress. Now, medicine is undergoing a big transformation,...

item.title: Helen Mirren subjected to abusive street confrontation in London involving alleged activist
item.description: The article reports on an incident in which actress Dame Helen Mirren was verbally abused during a street encounter in London. According to the report, she and her husband, director Taylor Hackford, w...

item.title: Himanta Biswa Sarma tells Gauhati High Court allegations rely on selective quotes from interviews
item.description: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has informed the Gauhati High Court that allegations of hate speech against him are being made on the basis of selective extracts taken from his interviews and...

item.title: Bishop Muthee Kiengei Mourns the Death of His Nephew Joseph Kamau
item.description: Bishop Muthee Kiengei of Jesus Christ Compassionate Ministries is currently mourning the sudden death of his nephew, Joseph Kamau, also known as Watoria. Kamau, who was the eldest son of Kiengei’s lat...

item.title: ACT Police respond to two small explosions in Kingston unit block
item.description: ACT Policing attended to reports of two small explosions in Kingston, Canberra, on Thursday, May 28. The first incident occurred around 3pm, with a second reported at 7.20pm at a unit block on Jardine...

item.title: Samkelo Mokhine discusses how the xenophobia label may affect debate on immigration and governance in South Africa
item.description: In this opinion piece, Samkelo Mokhine reflects on South Africa’s ongoing and highly sensitive debate around immigration and the use of the term “xenophobia” in public discourse. The article is framed...

item.title: Nigeria Customs seals tanker at Tin Can Island over document irregularities and unauthorised discharge
item.description: The Tin-Can Island Port Command of the Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) has sealed a vessel, MT NY Maria, following alleged documentation irregularities and unauthorised discharge of Premium Motor Spirit...

item.title: Economist flags risks and structural concerns in Philippines’ participation in Pax Silica semiconductor alliance
item.description: The article discusses the Philippines’ entry into Pax Silica, a US-led alliance aimed at strengthening supply chains for artificial intelligence, semiconductors, and critical minerals. While the agree...
